title: Terms & Conditions
s1_p1: Sílvia Seixas Pinho Peralta, trading under the brand Sweevil, NIF 247 911 780, registered address at Rua das Eirinhas, 157, Casa 6, 4300-166 Porto, Portugal.
s2_p1: These Terms and Conditions govern all purchases made in the Sweevil online store (sweevil.pt). By completing an order, the customer fully accepts these terms.
s3_p1: All prices shown include VAT at the applicable legal rate. Sweevil reserves the right to change prices without prior notice; the price applicable is the one in effect at the time of the order. Product images are for illustrative purposes only and may differ slightly from the actual items.
s4_p1: The following payment methods are accepted:
s4_p2: Payment is processed at the time of the order. If payment is not confirmed, the order will be automatically cancelled.
s5_p1: Orders are shipped throughout Europe via CTT, DPD, DHL, and GLS carriers. Delivery may take up to 8 business days, depending on the availability of the Sweevil brand, the destination, and the selected carrier.
s5_p2: The customer will receive a confirmation email once the item has been dispatched, with the corresponding shipping information.
s5_p3: Shipping costs are calculated at checkout based on the destination and order weight.
s6_p1: The customer has 14 calendar days from the date of receipt of the item to exercise the right to withdraw from the contract, without providing any reason. For more information, please see our
s7_p1: Returned items must be in perfect condition, unused, and in their original packaging. Return shipping costs are borne by the customer. Refunds are issued via the same payment method used for the purchase, within 14 days of receiving the returned item. For more details, please see our
s8_p1: In the event of a dispute, the consumer may contact an Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) entity. The European online dispute resolution platform is available at
s8_p3: You may also file a complaint through the Electronic Complaints Book at
s9_p1: These Terms and Conditions are governed by Portuguese law. For any disputes arising from the contractual relationship, the courts of Porto shall have jurisdiction, without prejudice to applicable consumer protection legislation.
